lessons.title: Classes Basics
lessons.concept_intro: A class is a blueprint for creating objects. It bundles data and functions together.
lessons.analogy: A cookie cutter (class) makes cookies (objects). All cookies have the same shape but can have different toppings (attributes).

lessons.title: Methods
lessons.concept_intro: Methods are functions that belong to a class. They can access and modify the object's data.
lessons.analogy: If a Dog is a class, then bark() and fetch() are its methods — things it can DO.

lessons.title: Attributes
lessons.concept_intro: Attributes are variables that belong to an object. Instance attributes are unique per object, class attributes are shared.
lessons.analogy: Every dog has its OWN name (instance attribute), but all dogs share the species 'Canis familiaris' (class attribute).

lessons.title: Inheritance
lessons.concept_intro: A class can inherit from another class, getting all its methods and attributes plus adding its own.
lessons.analogy: A SportsCar inherits everything from Car (engine, wheels, drive) but adds turbo and spoiler.

lessons.title: When to Use Classes
lessons.concept_intro: Classes aren't always needed. Use them when you have data AND behavior that belong together.
lessons.analogy: Use a class when you'd naturally say 'this THING can DO stuff' — a Player can move, a BankAccount can deposit.

project.title: Final Project (Your Choice!)
project.description: Build something YOU want using everything you've learned. Game, tool, bot — your call.
